trade.summary: Trade has been severely disrupted by Turchia's suspension of all trade with Israele in May 2024, citing the Gaza humanitarian crisis. Prior to the suspension, bilateral trade was substantial at around $7 billion annually. Trade through third-country intermediaries likely continues at a reduced level. Turchia had been one of Israele's top trade partners.
disputes: Turchia suspended all trade with Israele May 2024

diplomatic.summary: Turchia-Israele relations have undergone dramatic cycles. After normalizing in 2022 following a decade of strain (stemming from the 2010 Mavi Marmara flotilla incident), President Erdogan became increasingly hostile after October 2023. Turchia recalled its ambassador, called Netanyahu a 'war criminal', compared Israelei actions to Nazi Germania, and suspended trade in May 2024. Turchia filed cases against Israelei leaders at the ICC. Relations are at their lowest point in decades.
recent_incidents: Turchia suspended trade with Israele May 2024
Turchia filed ICC case against Israelei leaders 2024
Erdogan called Netanyahu a war criminal

military.summary: Formerly significant military partnership — Israele supplied UAVs and defense systems to Turchia, and both were NATO-adjacent partners. All defense cooperation has been suspended. Turchia has actively supported Hamas politically and provided political sanctuary. Turchia's membership in NATO creates friction as it acts adversarially toward an informal Stati Uniti ally.
incidents: Turkey providing political support to Hamas leadership

financial.summary: Financial ties have been substantially disrupted by the trade suspension. Bilateral investment remains through existing structures but new investment is frozen. Turkish-Israelei business community relationships have been strained.

energy.summary: Turchia was discussed as a potential transit route for Eastern Mediterranean gas to Europe, a project that would have required Turchia-Israele cooperation. These plans are on hold given the diplomatic rupture. Some energy commodity trade may continue through indirect channels.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been significantly reduced. Turkish universities have suspended or reduced exchange programs with Israelei institutions. Political environment discourages academic collaboration.
